It's still the same empty room.
If she passes the audition, she might be able to move to a better room.
She doesn’t seem particularly bothered by it, but this room is so unfitting for her.
The owner of the room
isn't visible,
but that works to my advantage. I don’t have a good impression of him.
All that's in the room is a single bed, and just a table and chairs. Is she really living with just this?
Now that the audition is over, she seems to have lost her worries. Today she has a refreshed look.
Even in this room, which is roughly unsuitable for her world, somehow she doesn't feel out of place.
Is it because of her talent as an actress that she blends seamlessly into any landscape?
In the room alone
Waiting for his return must be tough for a 16-year-old girl, right? She probably can't even walk around the city.
About that thing, the date and time have been set.
Really? So, when did we decide to meet?
We're meeting in front of the Guggenheim Museum at noon in a week.
Phew.
Hmm, it's a weird place to meet up, but that's fine.
Oh no, what should I do? I'm starting to feel all jittery.
Is he not here today either?
Well, the audition is technically over, but he just went off somewhere. It's so mean to leave such a cute girlfriend behind, right?
He might be mad, after all.
Because I made him audition for that band.
That can't be true. I think it was a good experience for him no matter what form it took.
I wish he could think like that.
I wonder when he'll come back.
Does Gloria not know this place?
I don't think she does, right? We haven't told her, and before that,
it's a different place than where he used to live.
It might be meddlesome, but if she has something to say, I think it's better to say it clearly for both of their sakes.
If they can understand each other, that would be the case.
But it's a bit of a delicate relationship for us.
Let's stop talking about Gloria already.
Ah.
Hmm, it's kind of thrilling.
I feel a bit complicated, like it's both fun and scary.
What if I found out my mother is none other than Meryl?
Shin
What would Shin do? Would he still quit his job?
I haven’t seen him lately.
Why isn’t he here even though the audition is over?
I can’t believe he’s arguing with her.
It seems there’s a rift between Gloria and me that I don’t understand.
That must be the reason for it.
Did he say he wants to quit his job?
If things aren't going well with Gloria, he should just look for another agent. It would be such a waste to ruin his career.
Meryl is
not saying good things about him.
It might be too much to introduce them without any background knowledge.
When Meryl and
eventually meet, what kind of impression will they give each other?
Come visit again, okay?
